L1.area: Server-side request handling and data processing pipeline

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled data from HTTP requests is deserialized without any validation or restriction when a specific serialization format is requested via a content type header, enabling unauthenticated remote code execution.

L3.area: Pickle-based data serialization subsystem and API endpoint request dispatching
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled data sent via HTTP requests using the pickle-based serialization content type is deserialized without any validation or restriction on the main API server, enabling unauthenticated remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The PickleSerde.deserialize_value method calls pickle.loads on untrusted HTTP request data without any validation, and the API endpoint handler does not restrict the pickle content type on the main server, allowing any unauthenticated user to achieve remote code execution.
